response.justification: Bogdan has a broad software background with genuine strengths in distributed systems and testing culture, but fails to meet the role's most critical minimum qualification: 8 years of C++ experience. His C++ exposure is limited to roughly 6 months at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a bachelor's thesis project, and C++ does not appear in his listed skills at all. His primary expertise is in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. While he has touched distributed systems and infrastructure at scale (Dunnhumby, Kubernetes at GISCollective, AWS CDK at Optilyz), this falls well short of the required 5 years of large-scale infrastructure/compute experience, and he has no formal technical leadership role in cloud or DevOps.
strengths: Demonstrated experience with high-throughput distributed systems at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second, showing practical understanding of low-latency, large-scale infrastructure

Deep commitment to engineering quality through TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and community knowledge-sharing (workshops, dojos, open source), aligning with the mentoring and best-practices aspects of the role
Broad polyglot experience across embedded, web, mobile, and cloud-adjacent tooling (Kubernetes, AWS CDK, Docker) indicates adaptability and architectural range
concerns: C++ experience is critically insufficient — approximately 6 months at National Instruments plus a thesis project versus the 8-year minimum requirement; C++ is absent from his skills section entirely

No formal technical leadership role in cloud infrastructure or DevOps; staff engineer titles are present but leadership scope appears to be individual contribution and informal mentoring rather than owning platform strategy

Primary technology stack (TypeScript/Node.js/DLang) is misaligned with the core technology requirement of the role; cloud platform depth is limited to peripheral tooling rather than production cloud architecture ownership
